title: deploy: handle non-JSON error responses — print raw text body
description: When the backend (or a proxy like APISIX) returns a non-JSON error response (plain text, HTML, empty body), deploy.js currently hits the 'Unexpected non-JSON response' branch and swallows the body entirely. Fix: always try to print the raw response body text on error, regardless of content type.

features: In deployHosted() in src/commands/deploy.js, replace the current response handling block (steps 5+) with the following pattern:
After receiving the response, if response.ok: try to parse as JSON and handle success as before.

If NOT response.ok: read the body as text first (response.text()), then attempt JSON.parse() on the text. If JSON.parse succeeds, use data.error || data.message || data.detail || the raw text as the error message. If JSON.parse fails, use the raw text directly as the error message.
This handles: FastAPI (returns JSON with 'detail' field), plain text errors from proxies, HTML error pages (show first 200 chars).
Add 'data.detail' to the field fallback chain — FastAPI uses 'detail' not 'error' or 'message'.
For 401: print error message first, then the hint on next line.
For --verbose: print the full raw text body regardless.
If body is empty string: print 'Deploy failed (HTTP <status>): (empty response body)'.

notes: The key insight: use response.text() first, then JSON.parse() optionally — NOT response.json() which throws on non-JSON. Also add data.detail to the field lookup chain since FastAPI error responses use {detail: string} not {error/message: string}.
